item.title: Key Russian Oil Port Resumes Loading Amid Attempt to Divert Flow
item.description: Russia’s top oil port in the Baltic Sea resumed loading days after it came under attack from Ukrainian drones, although the company that pipes crude there said it is trying to divert barrels elsewhere because of the incidents.

item.title: Crypto Enters the Mortgage Market Via Fannie Mae-Eligible Loans
item.description: Crypto is moving deeper into the US financial system — this time through the mortgage market.

item.title: South Africa Holds Rates Over Iran War Inflation Concerns
item.description: South Africa’s central bank held interest rates steady to assess the impact of the global oil-price shock caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Data-Center Shift From Middle East Would Test US Grid
item.description: Network operators are already scrambling to add capacity as demand from the tech industry soars.

item.title: Sonos’ New Play Speaker Shows the Company is Finally Back on Track
item.description: Sonos Inc. is back doing what it does best: putting out high-quality Wi-Fi speakers that look nice, sound good and work best together as a system.

item.title: Bertelsmann Profits Down With Declining Results of TV unit RTL
item.description: The outgoing chief executive officer of Germany’s biggest media company said the TV and streaming industries in Europe is facing a crisis and must consolidate.

item.title: Defense Startup Shield AI Nabs $2 Billion at $12.7 Billion Value
item.description: Shield AI has raised $2 billion at a valuation of $12.7 billion, more than doubling its value from a year ago, underscoring investor demand for defense technology and artificial intelligence.

item.title: US Continuing Jobless Claims Drop to Lowest in Almost Two Years
item.description: Recurring applications for US unemployment benefits fell to the lowest level in almost two years, indicating ongoing stability in the labor market despite fresh economic headwinds.

item.title: BofA Shifts Focus to Air Defense Firms Over Tank and Ammo Makers
item.description: Bank of America Corp. is picking new favorites in the European defense sector as the Iran war highlights the importance of missile systems and drones.

item.title: Canadian Oil Firm New West Weighs US IPO to Fund Advanced Computing Pivot
item.description: Canadian energy producer and Bitcoin miner New West Data is exploring a US initial public offering to help meet the growing need for powerful computing systems.

item.title: MetaX’s Sales More Than Double on Chinese AI Chip Demand
item.description: MetaX Integrated Circuits Shanghai Co.’s full-year revenue more than doubled, driven by robust domestic artificial intelligence demand while Nvidia Corp. is squeezed out of the Chinese market.

item.title: Holders of Russian Debt Gain Easier Path to Payment at Euroclear
item.description: A path is clearing for European holders of Russia’s foreign currency bonds to recover payments immobilized by international sanctions since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: How Japan Can Use the Oil Market to Support the Yen
item.description: Japan’s heavy reliance on imported oil means swings in crude prices feed quickly into its currency and trade balance. The government has hinted that it might step into the oil market in an indirect bid to support the weakening yen, as the war in the Middle East drives up energy costs and threatens the global economy.

item.title: WTO Meets Amid ‘Worst Industrial Crisis’ in Memory
item.description: Rules-based trade faces ‘defining moment’ in talks kicking off today amid energy crisis

item.title: Argentina’s Sluggish Economy Is Testing Milei’s Spending Cuts
item.description: Cracks emerging in Argentina’s economic growth are now threatening one of President Javier Milei’s signature achievements: the fiscal surplus.

item.title: Aligned Data Centers Closes on $2.58 Billion Credit Facility
item.description: Aligned Data Centers has raised $2.58 billion through a revolving credit facility to help the company finance its data center expansion in the US.

item.title: Total Goes on Mideast Oil-Buying Spree, Adding to Market Turmoil
item.description: The trading arm of French oil major TotalEnergies SE embarked on one of the biggest-ever buying sprees of Middle Eastern oil this month, helping to send prices soaring in a market already facing a liquidity squeeze because of the war.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Micron, MillerKnoll, Olaplex, Precigen
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.8% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as doubt grows about Iran’s willingness to engage in ceasefire talks.

item.title: US Futures Fall as Trump Urges Iran to Respond Before ‘Too Late’
item.description: US stock-index futures declined on Thursday as oil prices rose on conflicting views between the US and Iran over the peace talks.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Keeps 2026 Growth Forecast Despite Iran War
item.description: Brazil’s central bank maintained its economic growth forecast for this year despite higher uncertainty stemming from the Iran War, while warning that a more prolonged conflict would weigh on activity.

item.title: An 800-Year-Old Math Principle May Help Find Bottom to S&P 500’s Rout
item.description: The S&P 500 Index has clocked four consecutive weeks of declines and it’s on track for the worst month in a year.

item.title: Investors Switch to Cash From Stocks and Bonds Like It’s 2022
item.description: Investors avoiding risks from the Iran war are selling stocks and bonds and rebuilding cash allocations, in an echo of strategies followed after Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022.

item.title: Europe Bond Market Doubts Grow Over Recovery From War Selloff
item.description: Europe’s bonds will struggle to bounce back from a sharp selloff triggered by the war in the Middle East, even if the conflict comes to a swift conclusion, according to market players.

item.title: Iran War Just Canceled a Better Economic Outlook for the World
item.description: Imagine an alternative reality where Donald Trump opted against attacking Iran.

item.title: TikTok Plans Bigger, Bolder Actions With Future Secured
item.description: The threat of a US ban no longer overshadows the app

item.title: EU Targets Snapchat, Porn Websites in Child Protection Crackdown
item.description: The European Union opened an investigation into Snap Inc.’s social network and escalated a probe into a number of pornography platforms as the bloc ramps up efforts to protect children online.

item.title: Iceland’s Inflation Climbs, Paving Way for More Rate Hikes
item.description: Iceland’s inflation rate rose to the highest level in a year and a half, driven by the cost of oil and putting pressure on the island’s central bank to tighten its monetary stance even further.

item.title: BOE’s Breeden Plays Down Risk of Second-Round Inflation Effects
item.description: Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden played down the threat of a wage-price feedback loop in response to the Iran War, saying a weak economy means workers and firms have less bargaining power.

item.title: Germany Sees Danger of 2026 Growth Rate Halving on Iran Crisis
item.description: German officials see a risk that the nation’s economy will grow at just half the pace they had envisaged if the Iran war drags on, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Coca-Cola Bottler Raising Euro Bonds to Buy Africa Business
item.description: Coca-Cola HBC Finance BV is raising at least €1.5 billion ($1.7 billion) of debt on Thursday to help fund an acquisition of another drinks bottling firm.
